seoTitle: Venn vs Vernon Islands — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Venn and Vernon Islands. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Venn vs Vernon Islands: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Venn and Vernon Islands.
heading: Venn vs Vernon Islands — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Venn, Northern Territory and Vernon Islands, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Venn has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to Vernon Islands (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Venn or Vernon Islands safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Venn has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Vernon Islands scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Venn scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Vernon Islands scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Venn or Vernon Islands?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Venn has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
